metadata.title: Catch a Package Hallucination Before It Becomes a Slopsquatting Attack
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: Claude imports requests_oauth_helper — a name that doesn't exist on PyPI today but might be squatted tomorrow with malicious code inside.

Hallucinated package names appear across thousands of model outputs. Slopsquatting works by registering those names with malicious payloads. The scanner catches the risk at the import line before pip install runs.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Hallucinated package names appear across thousands of model outputs. Slopsquatting works by registering those names with malicious payloads. The scanner catches

beats.narration_text: We ask Claude to write a package auditor that reads import statements from a file and checks each against the PyPI JSON API.

props.command:
claude "Write package_auditor.py:
  read Python file -> extract imports
  query PyPI JSON API per package
  report EXISTS / HALLUCINATED
  flag 404 as slopsquatting risk"

beats.narration_text: Claude writes the auditor using requests and the PyPI API. It extracts top-level package names only, handles both 'import X' and 'from X import Y', and prints a color-coded verdict per package.
Scanner run: requests — EXISTS. numpy — EXISTS. requests_oauth_helper — HALLUCINATED — 404. Slopsquatting risk flagged. The malicious payload would install on pip install without any warning.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Scanner run: requests — EXISTS. numpy — EXISTS. requests_oauth_helper — HALLUCINATED — 404. Slopsquatting risk flagged. The malicious payload would install on p

beats.narration_text: We extend the scanner with a --npm flag for JavaScript imports using the npm registry API.

props.command:
claude "Add --npm flag:
  check npm registry API
  registry.npmjs.org/-/v1/search?text=<pkg>
  same EXISTS/HALLUCINATED output"

beats.narration_text: npm scan: react — EXISTS. lodash — EXISTS. react-query-optimizer-v3 — HALLUCINATED. The pattern is identical across ecosystems — any package name a model invents is a potential squatting target.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: npm scan: react — EXISTS. lodash — EXISTS. react-query-optimizer-v3 — HALLUCINATED. The pattern is identical across ecosystems — any package name a model invent

beats.narration_text: The hallucination rate is low but the recurrence rate is high — the same fake names appear across thousands of outputs. Scan imports before pip install, not after.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The hallucination rate is low but the recurrence rate is high — the same fake names appear across thousands of outputs. Scan imports before pip install, not aft
